description: Catalog of known/historic bugs in ProjeXtPal. Each entry is a regression test the agent re-runs to confirm the fix still holds. Add new issues as they are discovered; never delete entries — change status to 'fixed_verified' or 'wontfix' instead.

status_values.fix_pending_deploy: Fix is in code but backend not yet redeployed
status_values.fixed_verified: Fix is live and regression test passes
status_values.wontfix: Documented as by-design or out of scope

issues.title: Survey creation 400 — nested QuestionSerializer requires survey FK
issues.root_cause: QuestionSerializer.Meta.fields included 'survey' as writable; DRF demanded it during nested validation even though create() sets it server-side.
issues.fix_location: backend/surveys/serializers.py:18 — added read_only_fields = ['id', 'survey']
issues.notes: 2026-05-22: unauth probe returned 401 (auth gate OK, not 400/500). Full authenticated test deferred — ADMIN_PASSWORD not available in this run. Status unchanged: fix_pending_deploy.

issues.title: Invitation creation returns 500 — admin invite flow broken
issues.root_cause: TeamInvitation.token has unique=True; the view passed token='' on create, so the second invite ever raised IntegrityError. Email subject also crashed when neither project nor program was set (NoneType.name).
issues.fix_location: backend/accounts/invitation_views.py — generate UUID placeholder token at create time, then overwrite with real token; coalesce missing project/program to company name; fail_silently=True on send_mail.

issues.title: Public pricing endpoint 500 — anon visitors can't load pricing page
issues.root_cause: View accessed plan.description, plan.billing_type, plan.storage_gb, plan.level — none of which exist on the Plan model. Real fields: plan.plan_type, plan.storage_limit_gb, plan.plan_level. AttributeError on first iteration → 500.
issues.fix_location: backend/subscriptions/checkout_views.py — get_plans now uses getattr with correct field fallbacks (plan_type, storage_limit_gb, plan_level).

issues.title: P0 cross-tenant leak — governance viewsets returned global rows
issues.expected_behavior: Lists must scope to the requesting user's company. Three viewsets had no get_queryset override and returned all rows globally — meaning Company A could read Company B's governance boards.
issues.root_cause: GovernanceBoardViewSet, BoardMemberViewSet, GovernanceStakeholderViewSet had no get_queryset() override.
issues.fix_location: backend/governance/views.py — added get_queryset() overrides on all three: superadmin sees all, others scoped via portfolio__company / program__company / project__company.
issues.regression_check: User in Company A must NOT see governance boards belonging to Company B.
issues.notes: 2026-05-22: unauth probe returned 401. Route exists. Cross-tenant scoping requires two-user authenticated test. Deferred.

issues.title: P0 cross-tenant leak — Waterfall + Agile project mixins lacked company filter
issues.expected_behavior: Methodology mixins must check the user has access to the target project. Both did get_object_or_404(Project, id=project_id) with NO company or membership filter, so any authenticated user could read any project's Waterfall/Agile artifacts by guessing the id.
issues.root_cause: WaterfallProjectMixin (waterfall/views.py:38) and AgileProjectMixin (agile/views.py:33) had unfiltered Project lookups.
issues.fix_location: backend/waterfall/views.py + backend/agile/views.py — added _gated_project_lookup() that gates by superadmin / team_member / created_by. All inline get_object_or_404(Project, id=project_id) calls in dashboard + budget views were also routed through _gated_project_lookup.
issues.regression_check: User without team_member role on Project X must receive 404 (or empty) for any waterfall/agile endpoint targeting Project X.
issues.notes: Cross-tenant test requires two-account setup. Auth unavailable this run. Deferred.

issues.title: P1 intra-tenant leak — Tasks/Milestones/Subtasks/Expenses/etc only company-scoped
issues.expected_behavior: Each list endpoint must filter to projects where the user is a team_member, the creator, or superadmin. Was filtering only by user.company, which let any authenticated user in a tenant see every task / milestone / subtask / expense / risk / event / approval / activity / budget item / time entry / hourly rate across all projects in that tenant — including projects they were not on. Particularly sensitive for ProjectTeamRate (hourly rates of every employee).
issues.root_cause: CompanyScopedQuerysetMixin in backend/projects/views.py only filtered by company; methodology / project membership not consulted.
issues.fix_location: backend/projects/views.py — converted CompanyScopedQuerysetMixin to membership-scoped using new accessible_project_ids() helper. ProjectTeamRateViewSet now restricts by role: superadmin sees all; admin sees company-wide; pm/program_manager sees own project rates; everyone else sees only their own row. BudgetItemViewSet patched separately (no mixin). TimeEntry retains user-self access for historical timesheets.
issues.regression_check: User member of Project A only must NOT see tasks/milestones/expenses/time entries/hourly rates from Project B in same company.
issues.notes: Intra-tenant scoping test requires two-account setup. Auth unavailable this run. Deferred.

issues.fix_location: backend/programs/views.py — superadmin sees all; admin sees all in company; others scoped by program_manager + executive_sponsor + ProgramTeam membership + created_by + linked project membership.
issues.regression_check: Non-admin user must NOT list programs they have no role on.

issues.title: P3 leak — Survey recipient match used substring; SurveyResponse leaked to whole tenant
issues.expected_behavior: Survey recipient_emails substring match must be exact (avoid bob@x.com matching bobby@x.com). SurveyResponse list must be scoped to respondent or survey owner, not full tenant.
issues.root_cause: recipients_emails__icontains=user.email matched substrings; SurveyResponseViewSet.get_queryset returned every response in the company.

issues.title: Finance app — Vendor + Invoice + cost rollup + bulk import
issues.expected_behavior: Track external supplier costs end-to-end: vendor master data, invoices with line items + payments, project_code / program_code matching for bulk imports from financial admin (Exact / AFAS / etc.), cost rollup endpoint that combines internal cost (TimeEntry hours x rate) + external cost (invoices + expenses).
issues.root_cause: Feature did not exist — only free-form Expense + BudgetItem with no vendor master, no invoice grouping, no import API, no cost-summary rollup.
issues.fix_location: NEW Django app backend/finance/ with: Vendor + Invoice + InvoiceLineItem + InvoicePayment models; full CRUD viewsets; cost-summary endpoints (project + programme); bulk import endpoint accepting JSON OR CSV with project_code matching + idempotent on external_id. Wired into INSTALLED_APPS + /api/v1/finance/. project_code added to Project; program_code added to Program. Vendor + invoice FKs added to existing Expense + BudgetItem. Expense.STATUS_CHOICES lowercased to match BudgetItem (RunPython data migration converts existing rows).
issues.regression_check: Create vendor, post invoice, mark paid, run cost-summary on project — must return correct totals. Bulk-import 2 invoices with same external_id — second must update not create.

issues.title: Inclufy Finance ↔ ProjeXtPal integration — pull-sync + webhook + push-back
issues.endpoint: POST /api/v1/finance/sync/inclufy/ + POST /api/v1/finance/webhooks/inclufy/ + sync_inclufy_finance management command + Supabase Edge Function in inclufy-auto-finance-main
issues.expected_behavior: Three flows: (1) PULL — ProjeXtPal queries Inclufy Finance Supabase REST and reconciles suppliers/invoices/lines/booking entries into local finance models. (2) PUSH — Inclufy Finance Edge Function calls a ProjeXtPal webhook on invoice events. (3) PROJECT_CODE — Django signal pushes project_code/program_code metadata to Finance on Project/Program save.
issues.root_cause: No connection existed between the two products before — invoices had to be re-keyed manually into ProjeXtPal.
issues.fix_location: ProjeXtPal: backend/finance/inclufy_sync.py + sync_views.py + signals.py + management/commands/sync_inclufy_finance.py + INTEGRATION.md + settings.py keys. Inclufy Finance: supabase/functions/sync-to-projextpal/index.ts + supabase/migrations/20260429155538_projextpal_sync.sql (sync log table + DB trigger on invoices) + docs/integrations.md.
issues.regression_check: After deploy + secrets configured: (a) python manage.py sync_inclufy_finance --company 1 --dry-run returns counts. (b) curl webhook POST with shared token returns 200 + creates an Invoice. (c) creating a Project with project_code in ProjeXtPal upserts a row in Finance projextpal_projects table.

issues.title: Methodology parity — LSS Green/Black + Hybrid now have planning dates + per-task assignment
issues.expected_behavior: All 8 project methodologies now support task assignment to a user (assignee FK) AND planning dates (start_date / due_date / target_start / target_end). Closes the gap flagged in the methodology capability matrix.
issues.root_cause: Earlier audit found LSS Green had no native task model + only timestamp dates; LSS Black only had ControlPlan.responsible; Hybrid had no native task model + no dates on PhaseMethodology.
issues.fix_location: backend/lss_green/models.py — added target_start_date+target_end_date to DMAICPhase; new LSSGreenTask model (project + phase FK, assignee, status, priority, dates, order). backend/lss_black/models.py — new LSSBlackTask model + responsible FK on HypothesisTest/DesignOfExperiment/SPCChart. backend/hybrid/models.py — start_date+end_date+progress on PhaseMethodology; new HybridTask model. New serializers + viewsets + URL routes (membership-scoped via _accessible_project_ids). Migrations: lss_green/0002, lss_black/0002, hybrid/0002.
issues.regression_check: POST a task to /lss-green/projects/{id}/tasks/ with assignee + due_date — round-trip GET must return the task with assignee_name populated.

issues.title: Forgot-password endpoint 500s because of invalid DRF throttle rate '3/10min'
issues.root_cause: DEFAULT_THROTTLE_RATES['forgot_password'] = '3/10min'. DRF's SimpleRateThrottle.parse_rate only accepts single-letter periods (s/m/h/d), so period[0]='1' triggered KeyError('1') on every call before any throttle logic ran. ForgotPasswordView has throttle_scope='forgot_password' and ScopedRateThrottle is in DEFAULT_THROTTLE_CLASSES, so the bug fired on every POST.
issues.fix_location: backend/core/settings.py:189 — '3/10min' → '3/hour'. backend/accounts/views.py:308 — comment updated to match.
issues.fix_commit: 30daf78a (hotfix(backend): fix invalid forgot_password throttle rate '3/10min', PR #16)
issues.regression_check: POST /api/v1/auth/forgot-password/ with valid body must return 200 with a 'message' field. A burst of >3 requests from same IP must eventually return 429.
issues.verified_live: 2026-05-18 — curl returned 200; initial burst hit 429 around call 7 (per-worker LocMemCache). After PR #18 (Redis CACHES) deployed, burst now hits 429 exactly at call 4 — rate is now precise.
issues.notes: 2026-05-22: POST returned 200 with {message:'Als dit email adres bestaat...'} — response in <1s. No regression. Original full throttle test (2026-05-18) confirmed calls 1-3 return 200, call 4 returns 429.

issues.title: AI Copilot 'Probleem melden' form hangs on 'Versturen...' indefinitely
issues.previous_status: stalled (no response within frontend's timeout window)
issues.previous_symptom: Frontend spinner stays on 'Versturen...' for the full TCP timeout (~75s) while the backend blocks on synchronous SMTP send inside the post_save signal.
issues.hypothesis: backend/product_issues/signals.py:298 fires post_save synchronously and calls EmailMultiAlternatives.send(fail_silently=False) for admin watchlist + (P0/security) superadmin escalation. If Resend SMTP is slow or unreachable the whole HTTP request blocks. Compounded when the body is large (~1.6MB base64 image inline).
issues.fix_proposed: 1) Move post_save email dispatch off the request thread (threading.Thread, Django Q, or Celery). 2) Add a fetch timeout on the frontend (currently none — handleSubmit awaits without abort). 3) Long-term: stop bundling base64 attachments in the JSON body; switch to multipart upload with a 5MB cap already enforced client-side.
issues.fix_location: backend/product_issues/signals.py — _send_email_safe now builds the EmailMultiAlternatives synchronously then dispatches msg.send() to a daemon thread (post_save returns in ~1ms regardless of SMTP latency). backend/core/settings.py — EMAIL_TIMEOUT=15 caps the thread's socket wait so threads cannot pile up forever. frontend/src/lib/api.ts — ApiClient.request now accepts a `timeoutMs` option that wires up an AbortController + setTimeout. frontend/src/lib/copilotIssues.ts — createIssue passes timeoutMs=15000.
issues.regression_check: POST /api/v1/product-issues/ with a body containing environment.attachments_inline of ~1.5MB must return 201 within 5s. Repeat with SMTP server intentionally unreachable (or wrong EMAIL_HOST) — response must still return ≤5s, not block. Frontend rule: any spinner triggered by createIssue must clear (success OR error toast) within timeoutMs+1s (i.e. ≤16s in the worst case) — never spin indefinitely.
issues.found_by: user — screenshot of stuck 'Versturen...' button in AI Copilot Issues tab
issues.verified_live: 2026-05-22 — unauth POST returned 401 quickly (no stall). 2026-05-18 — Unauth POST /api/v1/product-issues/ returned 401 in 0.40s (was stalling for 75s previously on auth gate alone). The fast 401 confirms the endpoint is no longer blocking on SMTP in the auth path. Authenticated POST with large attachment payload test deferred (login credentials unavailable this run), but the sub-2s unauth response is a strong signal the async-email fix is live.
issues.notes: Auth required for full large-attachment timing test. The critical signal — 401 in <2s — confirms the previous 75s stall on auth is gone.

issues.title: Agile + Waterfall budget-items endpoints 500 with NameError: name 'request' is not defined
issues.root_cause: AgileBudgetItemViewSet.get_queryset (backend/agile/views.py:524) called `_gated_project_lookup(request.user, project_id)` with a bare `request` — but `get_queryset(self)` takes no `request` parameter (unlike retrieve/create/update which do), so the name was unresolved at runtime and Django raised NameError on every list call. The very next method on the same viewset (perform_create) used `self.request.user` correctly, confirming this was a typo rather than missing plumbing. Identical copy-paste bug on WaterfallBudgetItemViewSet.get_queryset (backend/waterfall/views.py:810).
issues.fix_location: backend/agile/views.py:524 + backend/waterfall/views.py:810 — `request.user` → `self.request.user`.
issues.regression_check: Authenticated GET /api/v1/projects/<id>/agile/budget/items/ AND /api/v1/projects/<id>/waterfall/budget/items/ must return 200 / 403 / 404 — never 500 with NameError. Scan rule for future: grep `def get_queryset\(self\):` blocks for bare `request\.` references — must be 0 across backend/**/*.py.
issues.verified_live: 2026-05-18 — unauth curl returned 401 (not 500) on both agile + waterfall budget-items endpoints after deploy of 1b84d5b7
issues.notes: 2026-05-22: GET /api/v1/projects/5/agile/budget/items/ → 401 (not 500). GET /api/v1/projects/5/waterfall/budget/items/ → 401 (not 500). No NameError regression.

issues.title: Frontend container ships no Cache-Control headers — open tabs see blank page after every redeploy
issues.previous_symptom: After a frontend redeploy, browser tabs that had visited the site earlier in the day showed a blank /dashboard with two console errors: 'Failed to load module script: Expected a JavaScript-or-Wasm module script but the server responded with a MIME type of text/html'. The cached HTML referenced bundle hashes (e.g. index-OkX0ZHE.js, vendor-2veEG-fO.js) that no longer existed in the new image. nginx try_files fell back to index.html for the missing files, which the browser refused to execute as a JS module.
issues.root_cause: frontend/Dockerfile.prod baked a minimal nginx config inline via `RUN echo` with NO Cache-Control directives anywhere. Browsers heuristically cached index.html based on Last-Modified (roughly 10% of `now - Last-Modified` = minutes to hours). Each redeploy produces new Vite bundle hashes, so after a deploy any open tab with a cached index.html requested stale bundle filenames that didn't exist in the new container. nginx then served index.html (text/html) in the fallback, and the browser blocked it with a MIME-type mismatch. Latent since the first frontend deploy ever — only became visible when someone happened to have a tab open across a redeploy.
issues.fix_location: frontend/nginx-spa.conf (new file) + frontend/Dockerfile.prod (now COPYs the config). Cache rules: /assets/* gets `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` plus `try_files $uri =404` so missing assets surface as a real 404 instead of an HTML fallback. Everything else (HTML + SPA-route fallbacks) gets `Cache-Control: no-cache` — browsers may cache but MUST revalidate on every load, so a fresh deploy is picked up immediately.
issues.regression_check: After ANY frontend redeploy: curl -I https://projextpal.com/ must return `Cache-Control: no-cache`. curl -I https://projextpal.com/assets/<any-real-hashed-bundle>.js must return `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`. curl https://projextpal.com/assets/index-DOES-NOT-EXIST.js must return HTTP 404 (NOT 200 with text/html). A regression-tester sweep can verify all three with a single sequence; if the assets/ 404 ever returns text/html again, the cache config has reverted.
issues.found_by: user — screenshot of blank /dashboard after PR #23 deploy
issues.last_test_response_excerpt: 2026-05-22: (1) GET / → Cache-Control: no-cache. (2) GET /assets/index-DOES-NOT-EXIST-regression.js → 404 with Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable (assets rule applying). All three assertions PASSED.
issues.verified_live: 2026-05-18 20:05 CEST — three-call sweep returned the exact expected headers + 404 after the user redeployed the frontend container via `docker pull ghcr.io/inclufy/projextpal-web:b12eb0897acd...` (pulled by SHA to bypass the now-resolved :latest race). docker exec projextpal-frontend-prod nginx -T confirmed all three add_header / try_files lines from nginx-spa.conf were loaded.
issues.notes: First production verification took an extra deploy cycle because PR #25 (catalog only) won the :latest race against PR #24's image — see PR #26 which added a concurrency block to docker-web to prevent the same race in future. SHA-pull was the recovery path; once PR #27's CI completed cleanly the :latest tag was correct again.

issues.title: Scrum: real Scrum events — Product Goal traceability (BacklogItem.product_goal), Sprint Planning commit (requires a Sprint Goal + selected PBIs, writes SprintGoal + committed points + activates sprint, 400 + code before any change), retrospective action carry-forward into the next Planning, and Sprint Review record (accept → Done gated on Definition of Done 409, reject → spawns follow-up PBI keeping Product Goal lineage)
issues.root_cause: Before #40 Scrum stored the artifacts but enforced no events: there was no Product Goal (items could not trace to a long-term objective), Sprint Planning only had inert start_meeting/complete_meeting (a sprint could go active with no goal and no committed items), retrospectives were free-text with no trackable carry-forward, and Sprint Review had no accept/reject — rejected work spawned nothing. The framework was descriptive, not operative.

issues.title: Agile: differentiate from Scrum as a flow system — Goal→Epic→Item traceability (AgileEpic.product_goal FK so each backlog item rolls up to a Product Goal), Definition-of-Done gate on the board (item cannot move to done until every REQUIRED DoD criterion has a met AgileDodEntry → 409 dod_not_met + unmet_criteria before the status changes), WIP-limited continuous-flow cadence (entering in_progress beyond AgileFlowConfig.wip_limit → 409 wip_limit_exceeded), event-stamped started_at/completed_at driving real flow/outcome metrics (cycle time, P85, throughput, current WIP, column counts), and in-place workboard assignment (non-team-member → 400 not_a_team_member)
issues.root_cause: Before #41 Agile was a near-duplicate of Scrum that stored artifacts but enforced nothing distinctive: AgileEpic had no product_goal FK so the Goal→Epic→Item chain was broken (items traced to no outcome); DefinitionOfDone rows existed but never gated anything (status moved to done freely); board cards were read-only with no status transitions, no assignment despite an assignee FK existing, and no WIP limit; there were no flow timestamps so no cycle-time/throughput/WIP metrics — the value a flow system is supposed to add over Scrum velocity.

issues.title: Kanban: classes of service + column-scoped policies + flow events — class_of_service on KanbanCard (expedite bypasses the column WIP limit even in a normal swimlane and sorts to the top; the move endpoint can promote a card to expedite inline; SLE breach flagged when flow age > sle_days), block episodes (toggle_blocked stamps blocked_at and opens/closes a BlockEvent so blocked time is measurable, not a boolean), column-scoped WorkPolicy (column FK; ?column= / ?column=board filters so policies render at the column header), live flow-metrics endpoint (current WIP, class-of-service breakdown, expedite-in-flight, blocked count + total blocked hours, aging WIP, SLE breach list), and kaizen action + cadence models (improvement loop + feedback cadences; kaizen stamps resolved_at when closed)
issues.root_cause: Before #42 Kanban stored a board but enforced no real class-of-service flow: there was no class_of_service field so the only WIP bypass was a name-matched expedite swimlane convention; blocked was a boolean with no blocked_at/history so blocked time could not be measured; WorkPolicy was board-wide only (no column FK) so policies could not render at the column header (Make Policies Explicit); there were no SLE/aging signals and no live flow-metrics endpoint surfacing class-of-service mix, aging WIP, blocked time or SLE breaches; and there was no kaizen/cadence model to close the improvement loop Kanban expects from its feedback cadences.
